URI: https://w3id.org/rec#Architecture
Label: Architecture
Definition: A designed/landscaped (or potentially designed/landscaped) part of the physical world that has a 3D spatial extent. E.g., a building site, a building, levels within the building, rooms, etc.

Ontology: th_building.owl (EXTENSION)
URI: https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/thinkhome/ontology/BuildingOntology.owl#Volume

Label: Budova

Concept: Class
Ontology: building.ttl (CORE)
URI: http://bimerr.iot.linkeddata.es/def/building#Space
Label: Space
Definition: A space represents an area or volume bounded actually or theoretically. Spaces are areas or volumes that provide for certain functions within a building.

Concept: Class
Ontology: brick.ttl (EXTENSION)
URI: https://w3id.org/rec#Architecture
Label: Architecture
Definition: A designed/landscaped (or potentially designed/landscaped) part of the physical world that has a 3D spatial extent. E.g., a building site, a building, levels within the building, rooms, etc.

Concept: Class
Ontology: th_building.owl (EXTENSION)
URI: https://www.auto.tuwien.ac.at/downloads/thinkhome/ontology/BuildingOntology.owl#Area
Label: Area
Definition: Specifies the area of the space or building. This value should be equal to the area enclosed by the physical boundaries of the space, as defined by the intersection of the horizontal plane at the highest floor height and the volumetric model's vertical interior surface planes. Building area should be the sum of space areas whose PeopleNumber element values are greater than zero.

Concept: Class
Ontology: building.ttl (CORE)
URI: http://bimerr.iot.linkeddata.es/def/building#Building
Label: Building
Definition: A building represents a structure that provides shelter for its occupants or contents and stands in one place. The building is also used to provide a basic element within the spatial structure hierarchy for the components of a building project (together with site, storey, and space).

Concept: Class
Ontology: s4inma_2019-04-30.n3 (CORE)
URI: https://saref.etsi.org/saref4bldg/Building
Label: Building
Definition: A building represents a structure that provides shelter for its occupants or contents and stands in one place. The building is also used to provide a basic element within the spatial structure hierarchy for the components of a building project (together with site, storey, and space).

Zones may contain other zones.keywords_usadas:
What a delightful challenge!

After analyzing the user's request "Zones may contain other zones", I've extracted the key concepts, normalized them ontologically, and included technical synonyms. Here's the optimized search list:

Zone, Container, Class, Type, Region, Area, Subzone, Enclosure, Enclave, Subset, Component, Part, Entity

These terms should help you navigate the realm of Web Semantics (OWL/RDF) and find relevant information on zones and their relationships!

entidades_recuperadas.texto:
Concept: Property
Ontology: building.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#containsZone
Label: contains zone
Definition: Relationship to the subzones of a major zone. A space zone could for instance be contained in a storey zone which is further contained in a building zone.

Concept: Class
Ontology: brick.ttl (EXTENSION)
URI: https://w3id.org/rec#Zone
Label: Zone
Definition: A sub-zone within or outside of a building defined to support some technology and/or use, e.g., an HVAC zone, a parking space, a security zone, etc.

Concept: Class
Ontology: brick.ttl (EXTENSION)
URI: https://brickschema.org/schema/Brick#Zone
Label: Zone
Definition: (1) a separately controlled heated or cooled space. (2) one occupied space or several occupied spaces with similar occupancy category, occupant density, zone air distribution effectiveness, and zone primary airflow per unit area. (3) space or group of spaces within a building for which the heating, cooling, or lighting requirements are sufficiently similar that desired conditions can be maintained throughout by a single controlling device.

Concept: Class
Ontology: bot.ttl (CORE)
URI: https://w3id.org/bot#Zone
Label: Zone
Definition: 区域是物理世界或虚拟世界的一部分，既位于现实空间中又可在3D空间中拓展。bot:Zone的子集包括bot:Site, bot:Building, bot:Storey, or bot:Space。bot:Zone实例可包含其他bot:Zone实例，从而可以对区域进行分组或细分。 同时，bot：Zone的实例也可以与其他bot：Zone实例相邻。最后，bot：Zone可以实例化为bot：Element的两个关系，它们可以包含在（bot：containsElement）中，或者与其相邻（bot：adjacentElement）。

Concept: Class
Ontology: dbo.rdf (CORE)
URI: http://www.google.com/digitalbuildings/0.0.1/subfields#Zone
Label: Zone
Definition: Region of building which is conditioned.

Buildings are areas with spatial 3D volumes

veredicto_final:
**Recomendación de Red**

Se recomienda la siguiente red de ontologías:

* `brick.ttl`
* `th_building.owl`
* `building.ttl`

**Justificación**

1. **`brick.ttl`**: Esta ontología cubre el requisito "Construction sites are areas with spatial 3D volumes", lo que es fundamental para describir los sitios de construcción y su relación con el espacio tridimensional.
2. **`th_building.owl`**: Esta ontología complementa a `brick.ttl` al agregar el requisito "Buildings are areas with spatial 3D volumes", lo que permite describir edificios como áreas espaciales con volumen tridimensional.
3. **`building.ttl`**: Esta ontología agrega la capacidad de describir zonas y su relación con otras zonas, lo que es útil para modelar estructuras complejas.

**Observaciones**

La cobertura de requisitos en esta red es alta, ya que se cubren los conceptos básicos de construcción y edificios. Sin embargo, hay un posible hueco en la descripción de objetos inanimados (como materiales o herramientas) que no están directamente relacionados con las ontologías recomendadas. Para cubrir este hueco, podría ser necesario agregar una ontología adicional que se centre en la descripción de objetos y su relación con los sitios de construcción.

En general, esta red de ontologías proporciona una buena base para describir y modelar conceptos relacionados con la construcción y edificios, pero puede requerir complementos adicionales para cubrir todos los requisitos.
